skill.name: youtube-search-api-skill
skill.description: Extract structured YouTube search data using BrowserAct API. Supports videos, shorts, channels, and playlists with no CAPTCHA or geo-restrictions.
skill.summary: Extract structured YouTube search results via BrowserAct API without browser automation complexity.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 58 patterns including network access, API key usage, and external script execution. After semantic evaluation, all findings are FALSE POSITIVES or expected API client behavior. The script is a legitimate BrowserAct API client that makes authenticated HTTP requests to extract YouTube search data. No malicious intent, data exfiltration, or command injection vectors were confirmed.

low_findings.title: API Key Access via Environment Variable
low_findings.description: Script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment. This is standard API client practice, not a security vulnerability. The key is used only for authenticating with BrowserAct API and is never exposed or exfiltrated.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard API authentication pattern - key used only for Bearer token in Authorization header to BrowserAct API

low_findings.title: Network Requests to BrowserAct API
low_findings.description: Script makes HTTP requests to https://api.browseract.com/v2/workflow. This is the expected API endpoint for the service, not a data exfiltration vector.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Legitimate API client behavior - all requests go to the official BrowserAct API endpoint documented in SKILL.md

content.user_title: Search YouTube and Extract Video Data via API
content.value_statement: Scraping YouTube manually is slow and error-prone. This skill extracts clean structured data from YouTube search results using a reliable API service.
actual_capabilities: Search YouTube by keywords and extract structured video, shorts, channel, or playlist data
Filter results by type: Videos, Shorts, Channels, or Playlists
Retrieve metadata including title, description, view count, and publish date
Get direct URLs for each search result item
Set result limit up to 100 items per search
No CAPTCHA, IP blocks, or rate limiting issues
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key to function
Search performance depends on BrowserAct service availability
Cannot access private videos or age-restricted content

use_cases.title: Content Creator Research
use_cases.description: Find and analyze trending videos on specific topics to inform your content strategy. Extract view counts and titles to identify popular formats.

use_cases.title: Competitor Analysis
use_cases.description: Search for competitor brand names and extract their top-performing videos. Gather channel URLs and video metrics for benchmarking.

use_cases.title: Tutorial Aggregation
use_cases.description: Search for how-to videos on specific tools or software. Build a curated list of educational content for team training or documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Video Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search YouTube for videos about {topic} and show me the top 20 results with their view counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to find relevant videos on a topic

prompt_templates.title: Channel Discovery
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube channels related to {niche} and extract their channel URLs and video counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to discover creators in a specific area

prompt_templates.title: Shorts Monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for YouTube Shorts with the hashtag {hashtag} and show me the trending short videos.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to track trending short-form content

prompt_templates.title: Playlist Research
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube playlists about {subject} and list all the videos they contain with their descriptions.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants to explore curated content collections

output_examples.output:
Found 5 videos:
1. Automate Your Workflow with Python - 125K views - 2 days ago
2. Python Scripting for Beginners - 89K views - 1 week ago
3. Python RPA Tutorial - 67K views - 2 weeks ago
4. Python Automation Ideas - 45K views - 3 weeks ago
5. Automate Everything with Python - 34K views - 1 month ago

output_examples.input: Find YouTube channels about machine learning
output_examples.output:
Discovered 3 channels:
1. ML Mastery - 120 videos - 45K subscribers
2. AI Explained - 89 videos - 32K subscribers
3. Data Science Daily - 67 videos - 21K subscribers

best_practices: Provide specific keywords for more relevant results
Check that BROWSERACT_API_KEY is set before running searches
Start with smaller limits to verify data quality before scaling
anti_patterns: Do not use vague keywords like 'stuff' or 'things'
Do not exceed reasonable limits as results may be less accurate
Do not use this skill for scraping private or restricted content

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a BrowserAct API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running searches.

faq.question: What types of content can I extract?
faq.answer: You can extract Videos, Shorts, Channels, and Playlists. Choose the type that matches your search goal.

faq.question: How many results can I get per search?
faq.answer: You can request up to 100 results per search. Start with smaller limits to verify data quality.

faq.question: Will I get blocked by YouTube for too many requests?
faq.answer: No. The BrowserAct API handles rate limiting and CAPTCHA challenges automatically.

faq.question: Can I search for private videos?
faq.answer: No. This skill only extracts publicly visible YouTube search results.

faq.question: What data fields are included in the results?
faq.answer: Results include title, description snippet, view count, publish date, and direct URL for each item.
